Literature Title: Evaluating Disparities in Elderly Community Care Resources Using a Geographic Accessibility and Inequality Index
Study Area & Country: Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Minnesota, Ohio, Wisconsin, six states in the Midwestern United States
Accessibility Method: ESRD patients were categorized into five administrative MSA strata based on organ transplant volumes: high (MSAs with ≥20 transplants per year), medium-high (10–19 transplants per year), medium (5–9 transplants per year), medium-low (2–4 transplants per year), and low (≤1 transplant per year). Spatial accessibility was calculated using the distance between ESRD patient ZIP codes and transplant centers, employing driving time as the metric.
Facility Type: End-stage renal disease (ESRD) clinics and kidney transplant centers
Dist/Time Calc Method: Network-based origin–destination matrix using road network data and the Origin Destination Cost Minimization (ODCM) method in ArcGIS 10.2
